problem: Define the stable interface before implementation so callers and tests do not drift.

callers.name: plan skill
callers.need: Needs a stable contract section to reference from plan tasks.

public_surface.name: interface-spec.json
public_surface.signature_or_shape: schema-backed artifact with callers, surfaces, invariants, options, and chosen option

invariants: The interface spec is derived from brief and plan artifacts, not invented in isolation.
compatibility_constraints: Do not add a new canonical /forgeflow:design stage.

error_behavior.condition: Only one interface option is provided
error_behavior.expected_behavior: Validation fails because tradeoff exploration was skipped.

migration_concerns: Existing tasks without interface-spec.json remain valid unless the helper is explicitly invoked.

options.summary: Emit a JSON interface spec plus a contracts.md section.
options.shape: Write interface-spec.json as validation source and mirror the chosen public contract into contracts.md.
options.compatibility_notes: Compatible because contracts.md remains the operator-facing contract.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Markdown alone is too easy to desynchronize from tests and validators.

contracts_output.markdown_section:
## Interface Spec

The implementation must preserve the JSON interface-spec artifact.

decision_note: Use JSON plus contracts.md because it gives both deterministic validation and readable operator context.
